'''Rod Doyle''' is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s. He played for [[Sydney Roosters|Eastern Suburbs]], [[St. George Dragons|St. George]] and [[South Queensland Crushers|South Queensland]] in the NSWRL/ARL competitions and for [[Sheffield Eagles|Sheffield]] in the [[Super League]]

==Background==
Doyle is the younger brother of former professional rugby league player [[Jeff Doyle (rugby league)|Jeff Doyle]].

==Playing career==
Doyle made his first grade debut for St. George in round 11 of the [[1990 NSWRL season]] against [[Western Suburbs Magpies|Western Suburbs]]. Doyle only played seven games for St. George before transferring to Eastern Suburbs. Doyle played a total of ten games with Easts before signing with the newly admitted South Queensland team for the [[1995 ARL season]]. Doyle made his club debut for South Queensland in round 22 against defending premiers [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]] which ended in a 58-4 loss. The following year, Doyle played six games for the club as they finished with the [[Wooden Spoon (award)|Wooden Spoon]]. In 1997, he signed for English side Sheffield. Doyle played at lock for Sheffield in their [[1998 Challenge Cup]] final victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. It is considered one of the biggest upsets in the competitions history. Doyle played a total of 68 games for Sheffield and departed the club at the end of the 1999 season.<ref>Alan Whiticker/Glen Hudson: The Encyclopedia of Rugby League Players. (1995 edition) {{ISBN|1875169571}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.roosters.com.au/history/our-players/past-players/|title=Club Player Honour Roll|website=www.roosters.com.au}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/52552500|title=John Kear recalls when Sheffield Eagles stunned Wigan in final|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
